description: Cross-binding conformance for §6.6.5 inline-test assertions on a RANK-2 array OBSERVED whose expression references a model PARAMETER. The asserted variable `scaled` = k * base is a state-free computed observed of shape [rows, cols] with parameter k (default 1.5); each binding materializes it over the field's Cartesian index sets — with model parameters (load-time constants) in scope, but state NOT — and evaluates `reduce` collapses (max/min) and `coords` point-samples. This guards the build-time cellwise-evaluation parameter-binding fix: previously the Julia observed-assertion path re-materialized the observed via evaluate_cellwise with only the const-array registry in scope, so a parameter-dependent observed asserted directly raised E_TREEWALK_UNBOUND_VARIABLE. Python (setup_arrays materialized with param_values) and Rust (setup_arrays materialized with param_vec) already bound parameters in this path, so all three must agree on the assertion actuals. Julia is the reference binding: its `run_pde_tests` minted golden/observed_param_rank2.json.

scope_excluded.go: rewrite-only port; no makearray lowering / simulator / inline PDE-test runner
scope_excluded.typescript: rewrite-only port; no makearray lowering / simulator / inline PDE-test runner
